content.full_text:
State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
Barbara A. Milman 
Date 
July 23, 1985 
No. M-85-l63 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES CO~~,}~N') 
Diane Maura Fishburn L~I 
,~ 
Subject: 
Personal Loans to Legislators Made oy Major Donor/Lobbyist 
Employer 
FACTS: 
The majority shareholder of a close corporation has been a 
major donor for two years. 
Recently he/the corporation became a 
lObbyist employer. 
He has made personal loans to legislators 
(interest only, callable at will) to be used for business and 
real estate investments. 
The money does not go to the 
legislators' campaign committees. 
As far as he knows, these 
loans have been disclosed on the legi 
tors' SEls. 
QUESTION 
How should these loans be reported? 
ADVICE: 
The loans should be reported on the lobbyist employer report 
as activity expenses if made during the period covered by the 
statement. 
The outstanding loans do not have to be reported on a 
continuing basis. 
COMMENT: 
We should cons 
regulations. 
this situation when we amend our lobbying 


or James H. Nills 
-
13th Street 
filar, C A 
9 2 0 14 
Senator Wadie P. Deddeh 
State Capitol 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
,~. 
Dan Stanford, Chairman 
FPPC

Sacramento, CA 
95804
